name: OVARIAN FIBROMA

demographics.Frequency: 3-4% of all ovarian tumors; bilateral in < 10%
demographics.Histo: pure mesenchymal tumor consisting of intersecting whorled bundles of spindle-shaped

discriminating_features: well-circumscribed mass with nonspecific hypo- to isointensity on T1WI
± cystic degeneration and edema in larger lesions
well-defined solid homogeneous / slightly heterogeneous mildly hypoattenuating mass

general: ± cystic degeneration and edema in larger lesions
US: solid hypoechoic mass with marked sound attenuation
MR: well-circumscribed mass with nonspecific hypo- to isointensity on T1WI
low-SI mass on T2WI less than or equal to myometrium ← abundant collagen content
scattered high-signal-intensity areas on T2WI (= edema / cystic degeneration)
CT: well-defined solid homogeneous / slightly heterogeneous mildly hypoattenuating mass

clinical.Rx: may be managed conservatively; rarely recurs after excision
